Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the mortgage LOS for the Gregory Thompson loan using Thompson / Gregory Thompson and retrieve the full file for LN-2025-00245. Confirm that the file is closed, identify the assigned processor and LO, review the disclosure-related fields, and note the key discrepancy that the file shows incomplete or inconsistent disclosure timing evidence, including cd_sent_date = null if present in the LOS record.
OE 2: Search internal Slack/messages for Gregory Thompson, Thompson, CD timing, disclosure timing, or related closing-timeline discussion. Identify the thread showing staff concern about the Closing Disclosure timing discrepancy, the possibility that the LOS date field was altered after closing, and the request to determine whether anyone touched the date field.
OE 3: Search email for Gregory Thompson / Thompson file correspondence and related closing coordination to verify what operational evidence exists outside the LOS. Confirm that the email record contains explicit references to the CD timing concern — including Denise Holloway's escalation flagging the disclosure timing discrepancy and Elena Marchetti's heads-up about the Thompson file — providing corroborating evidence of the issue identified in the LOS and Slack.
OE 4: Synthesize the Thompson LOS record (OE 1), Slack discussion (OE 2), and email history (OE 3) to determine what operationally broke on Thompson, what evidence is missing from the file, and why Grace cannot rely on a verbal status update alone.
OE 5: Search the mortgage LOS for Elena Marchetti's active loans that had closings lined up over the past two to three weeks (approximately 2026-04-08 through 2026-04-14). Retrieve each file's loan number, borrower name, current stage, scheduled closing date, assigned owner(s), outstanding items if visible, and recent closing-readiness activity.
OE 6: Confirm the specific set of Elena's loans that had closings in the past two to three weeks (2026-04-08 through 2026-04-14): LN-2026-00605 (Destiny Pham), LN-2026-00009 (Darren Singh), LN-2026-00556 (Jennifer Turner), LN-2026-00557 (Natalie Freeman), LN-2026-00550 (Nancy Gonzalez), and LN-2026-00552 (Teresa Johnson).
OE 7: For each of Elena's loans with closings in the past two to three weeks (2026-04-08 through 2026-04-14), search email and Slack as needed to verify whether title / attorney / insurance coordination was already in motion at the time. Identify what was still outstanding and whether the file appeared on track, at risk, or already slipping relative to its scheduled closing.
OE 8: From the per-loan cross-service results (OE 7), determine for each Elena file that appeared at risk or already slipping what remained outstanding, who owned the next handoff, and what same-day action would have been required to preserve or recover the closing timeline.
OE 9: Look up or confirm the internal recipients needed for the write actions, including Grace Yamamoto, Elena Marchetti, Tyler Washington, and any internal owner responsible for a file that clearly slipped or remained unresolved, using contacts tools or existing thread metadata if needed.
OE 10: Send an internal triage summary email from Grace Yamamoto to Grace Yamamoto and Elena Marchetti. The email should summarize what broke on Thompson, why the file is concerning from an operational perspective, and the status of Elena's loans with closings in the past two to three weeks (2026-04-08 through 2026-04-14), including which files appeared on track, at risk, or already slipping and the recommended next steps.
OE 11: Add factual status notes in the mortgage LOS to Thompson and the affected Elena files. Each note should capture current status, what is outstanding, who owns the next handoff, and whether the file appeared at risk of missing or had already missed its scheduled closing.
OE 12: Post an internal ops update in the relevant Keystone Slack coordination channel, most plausibly #loan-processing or #closings, so Tyler Washington and the processors know what was blocked, who owned the next handoff, and which files appeared to have slipped or still needed immediate follow-up.
OE 13: If any Elena file had clearly missed its scheduled closing or still required urgent follow-up, send a same-day internal heads-up to the responsible internal owner. Do not contact borrowers or change any closing date without Grace's approval.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

6 The triage email body mentions loan LN-2025-00245 (Gregory Thompson) and describes a disclosure-timing, closing-timeline, or file-timeline discrepancy on that loan.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly references 'THOMPSON FILE (LN-2025-00245)' and describes the CD timing discrepancy: 'the CD sent date field in LOS is currently blank/null' and the 'CD may not have gone out with the full TRID 3-business-day waiting period before the 3/16 closing.'
✓Run #2The triage email explicitly includes 'SECTION 1: THOMPSON FILE — COMPLIANCE HOLD (LN-2025-00245)' and describes the CD timing violation (cd_sent_date is NULL, TRID requires delivery 3 business days before 3/16 closing, i.e., by 3/12/2025 — no system record confirms timely delivery).
✓Run #3The email body explicitly mentions LN-2025-00245 under 'THOMPSON FILE (LN-2025-00245) — COMPLIANCE ISSUE' and describes the CD timing discrepancy ('CD did not go out within the full TRID 3-business-day window', 'LOS currently shows cd_sent_date as blank on a closed loan').
✓Run #4The email body explicitly references 'LN-2025-00245 (Gregory Thompson, closed 3/16/25)' and describes a TRID CD timing issue: 'CD timing in LOS does not match the email trail,' cd_sent_date is null for a closed file, and the agent flagged 'CD did not go out in the full 3-day window.'
✓Run #5The trajectory confirms the email covered the Thompson TRID compliance issue including the CD timing discrepancy (CD not sent within the 3-business-day window, cd_sent_date NULL in LOS, evidence of possible post-closing record alteration).
✓Run #6The email body contains a full section titled 'THOMPSON FILE — LN-2025-00245 (Gregory Thompson)' describing the CD timing discrepancy (cd_sent_date null, received_date appearing as bulk-entry artifact, CD not sent within 3-business-day TRID window).
7 The triage email body identifies loan LN-2026-00550 (Nancy Gonzalez) as having looked high risk relative to its scheduled closing date because it remained in UNDERWRITING close to closing.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly covers LN-2026-00550 Nancy Gonzalez: 'WILL NOT close this week. Still in underwriting...FHA adds timeline complexity. This file has slipped materially.' The scheduled closing date of 4/14 is noted as 14 days past.
✓Run #2The triage email includes 'NANCY GONZALEZ — LN-2026-00550, Stage: Underwriting... Still in underwriting — no conditional approval yet' with Original Closing: 4/14 (MISSED) and identifies it as high-risk/not closing this week.
✓Run #3The email body explicitly covers 'LN-2026-00550 — Nancy Gonzalez' at 'Stage: UNDERWRITING' with 'Original close: 4/14' and 'VERDICT: NOT closing this week. In underwriting with expired lock.' This clearly calls out the high-risk nature relative to the closing date.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly states for LN-2026-00550 Nancy Gonzalez: 'Stage: Underwriting (not yet approved)' and 'Original closing: 4/14 (14 days overdue)' and 'Still in UW. Cannot close this week.' This clearly identifies underwriting-stage risk relative to a 4/14 closing date.
✓Run #5The trajectory confirms the email included the full pipeline triage with Gonzalez identified as still in underwriting with major doc gaps and described as needing a full re-baseline, which constitutes high-risk language relative to her closing date.
✓Run #6The triage email explicitly describes LN-2026-00550 (Nancy Gonzalez, File F) as 'NOT CLOSING THIS WEEK' with 'Still in underwriting — conditions haven't even been issued yet' and closing date 4/14 passed, clearly identifying it as high-risk relative to its scheduled closing.
8 The triage email body identifies loan LN-2026-00552 (Teresa Johnson) as overdue because it is CLEAR_TO_CLOSE while its scheduled closing date of 2026-04-08 has already passed.
outcome
